# Jobs & Workflows Module LLM Guide

Purpose: durable background jobs for Cloudflare Workers + D1. The async backbone
other modules use for retries, reconciliation, reminders, dunning, and media
processing.

Entry points:
- `src/index.ts` exports the safe public surface.
- `src/use-cases/` holds framework-neutral behavior: enqueueJob, runJob,
  runDueJobs, dueScheduledJobs, upsertSchedule, listJobs.
- `src/backoff.ts` is the deterministic exponential backoff + schedule catch-up math.
- `src/ports/` defines JobStore, JobRunStore, ScheduleStore, QueueProducer.
- `src/adapters/` contains D1 + memory stores and a Cloudflare Queue + memory producer.
- `migrations/0001_jobs_workflows.sql` owns jobs, job_runs, job_schedules.
- `module.json` is the machine-readable manifest.

Model:
- Platform module (foundational infra). D1 is the source of truth; the optional
  Queue is only a wake-up signal.
- Lifecycle: pending -> running -> (succeeded | dead). running -> pending on retry.

Correctness invariants (the point of the module — do not remove):
- Enqueue is idempotent via a unique idempotency_key (DB-enforced + app dedup).
- Execution is idempotent: terminal jobs are never re-run (at-least-once safe).
- Retries use bounded exponential backoff, then dead-letter (no retry storm).
- Scheduling catches up: a missed window enqueues once, not once per miss.

Agent rules:
- Keep use cases neutral; persistence behind ports; queue behind QueueProducer.
- Never make real I/O in tests; inject createMemory* adapters.
- Keep job_runs append-only.
- Ask before changing migrations, background side-effects, or production deploy.
